title: GU Embryology: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of GU Embryology
Identify key diagnostic features of GU Embryology
Apply clinical reasoning to GU Embryology management
Recognize common pitfalls in GU Embryology

sections.title: Overview of GU Embryology
sections.content:
**GU Embryology**

Mesonephric vs paramesonephric duct development, sexual differentiation, common anomalies (hypospadias, cryptorchidism, inguinal hernia)

**Key Points:**
• Wolffian (mesonephric) duct → male internal genitalia (epididymis, vas deferens, seminal vesicles)
• Müllerian (paramesonephric) duct → female internal genitalia (fallopian tubes, uterus, upper vagina)
• SRY gene → testis determining factor → testosterone + AMH

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Wolffian (mesonephric) duct → male internal genitalia (epididymis, vas deferens, seminal vesicles)
• Müllerian (paramesonephric) duct → female internal genitalia (fallopian tubes, uterus, upper vagina)

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• SRY gene → testis determining factor → testosterone + AMH
• AMH causes Müllerian duct regression
• 5α-reductase converts testosterone to DHT (external genitalia virilization)

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for GU Embryology:**

• SRY gene → testis determining factor → testosterone + AMH
• AMH causes Müllerian duct regression
• 5α-reductase converts testosterone to DHT (external genitalia virilization)

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of GU Embryology?

options.text: Wolffian (mesonephric) duct → male internal genitalia (epididymis, vas deferens, seminal vesicles)
options.explanation: Wolffian (mesonephric) duct → male internal genitalia (epididymis, vas deferens, seminal vesicles)

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

mcqs.question: What is the KEY diagnostic or management consideration for GU Embryology?

options.text: 5α-reductase converts testosterone to DHT (external genitalia virilization)
options.explanation: 5α-reductase converts testosterone to DHT (external genitalia virilization)

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases

mcqs.question: A patient presents with findings consistent with GU Embryology. Which finding would you expect?

options.text: Müllerian (paramesonephric) duct → female internal genitalia (fallopian tubes, uterus, upper vagina)
options.explanation: Müllerian (paramesonephric) duct → female internal genitalia (fallopian tubes, uterus, upper vagina)
